goal: 实现 fork contributor actor log 显式导入

summary.title: Import fork contributor actor logs
summary.what: Added an explicit upstream maintainer flow for accepting a fork contributor's Mainline actor log, including a new actor_log.accepted provenance event, domain view provenance, a mainline actor import CLI command, Hub display of accepted actor-log trust metadata, and tests for accepting contributor intent after a maintainer backfill already pinned the same PR merge commit. The earlier Hub external contribution path remains only a not-author-sealed fallback when no contributor actor log is available.
summary.why: A merged fork PR can carry author-sealed Mainline intent metadata in the contributor's fork actor ref even when upstream normal sync cannot see it. Upstream needs an explicit fetch/accept trust boundary so those actor-log events become part of the normal Mainline view and pin/coverage flow, without pretending GitHub PR body text is a sealed intent.
summary.user_goal: 实现 fork contributor actor log 显式导入

decisions.chose: Add mainline actor import as an explicit maintainer-accepted actor-log fetch/accept path.
decisions.rationale: Normal sync should not automatically trust arbitrary fork refs, but upstream maintainers need a durable way to merge author-sealed actor metadata once they choose to trust it.
rejected: Treat Hub external contribution records as the main solution
Parse GitHub PR body templates as Mainline intent

decisions.point: Pin and coverage interaction with maintainer backfill
decisions.chose: Reuse existing Pin/upsert note behavior so accepted contributor intents and maintainer backfills can coexist on the same merge commit.
decisions.rationale: Maintainer backfill is a rescue record and contributor actor-log intent is the author's sealed record; multiple intent refs on one commit are valid and coverage remains commit-level.

decisions.chose: Record actor_log.accepted in the accepting maintainer's actor log and project it into IntentView.Provenance/HubIntent.Provenance.
decisions.rationale: Readers need to see the author, source ref/remote, importer, and verified/author-sealed status without mutating the contributor's own sealed event.

decisions.chose: Do not import fork git notes; regenerate upstream pin notes through upstream Pin/PinExplicit.
decisions.rationale: Git notes are evidence about upstream main commits and fork notes may point at fork-only commits or stale merge guesses.

rejected.alternative: Only show imported GitHub PR metadata in Hub
rejected.reason: That explains the PR to a human but does not transport or merge the contributor's author-sealed Mainline actor log into upstream state.

rejected.alternative: Automatically fetch all fork actor logs during sync
rejected.reason: Fork refs cross a trust boundary and should require an explicit maintainer accept step.

rejected.alternative: Accept actor_log.accepted events from the fork source log
rejected.reason: Acceptance is an upstream trust decision and must be generated by the accepting upstream actor, not inherited from the fork.

review_notes: Validation: go test ./internal/engine -run 'TestImportActorLog|TestReconcile|TestPin|TestBackfill|TestCoverage' -count=1; go test ./internal/hub -count=1; go test ./internal/cli -count=1; go run . actor import --help; git diff --check.

The test fixture covers a maintainer backfill already pinned to the fork PR merge commit, then accepts contributor actor log and verifies the merge note retains both intent refs while the contributor intent is merged and not left in the review queue.
Full go test ./... was not rerun in this final pass; earlier full attempts on this branch timed out in an existing unrelated internal/engine property test.

architectural_claims: Fork contributor Mainline metadata enters upstream through explicit actor-log accept, not through Hub-only display or PR-body parsing.
Accepted actor-log provenance is domain state on IntentView, so CLI/Hub/JSON read the same trust metadata.
Upstream pin notes remain the source for coverage and merged status; importing fork actor logs does not import fork notes.
behavioral_changes: mainline actor import can fetch a contributor actor ref from a fork remote or accept an already-fetched import ref, validate actor ownership, update the upstream actor ref, write an accept event, rebuild view, auto-pin, and push accepted metadata when upstream remote exists.
Hub intent pages display accepted_actor_log provenance with author-sealed, verified, accepted-by, and source ref details.
Hub external_contributions remain not-author-sealed fallback records and do not affect actor counts, review queues, coverage, or pin logic.

data_model_changes.name: IntentProvenance
data_model_changes.migration_notes: Existing views omit provenance; rebuild fills it only when actor_log.accepted events exist.

data_model_changes.name: ActorLogAcceptedEvent
data_model_changes.migration_notes: New event is additive and ignored by older readers that skip unknown event types.

security_implications: Fork actor refs are not trusted by normal sync; actor import requires an explicit maintainer action and validates every imported event actor_id matches the requested actor.
Foreign actor_log.accepted events in the source actor log are rejected so acceptance provenance is generated only by upstream.
Import does not parse GitHub PR body templates and does not import fork notes.
migration_notes: Teams with existing maintainer backfills can later import contributor actor logs; both intent refs may coexist on the same merge commit note.
